problem: The existing implementation exposes subscription plans and a paid-subscription read model, but it does not provide a complete client purchase lifecycle, governed renewal, cancellation, expiry, compensation readback, or same-commit evidence. A plan or subscriber count must never be treated as financial truth without WLT evidence.
goals: Create and govern subscription plans and benefits without duplicating financial ownership.
Allow an authenticated client to purchase an active plan through a WLT-bound payment session.
Activate only after captured-payment proof matches client, tenant, product, amount, currency and DSH purchase reference.
Expose the effective entitlement, lifecycle status and allowed actions to the client from WLT truth.
Support governed renewal, cancellation, expiry and compensation tracking.
Append an auditable WLT loyalty entry for the configured activation award exactly once.
Prevent subscriber or revenue claims when WLT evidence is missing, stale or inconsistent.

notAffected.app-partner: Partners do not own client paid subscriptions in JRN-027.
notAffected.app-captain: Captains have no subscription action or entitlement in JRN-027.
notAffected.app-field: Field workers have no subscription action or entitlement in JRN-027.
notAffected.webapp: No authenticated client subscription runtime is registered for this surface.
notAffected.website: Public website may market plans later but is not an authenticated purchase surface in this journey.

dshOwns: marketing plan labels and non-financial benefits
wltOwns: subscription activation, period, cancellation and expiry
active subscriber and recurring revenue summaries
prohibitedDuplication: DSH must not store wallet balances, ledger entries, provider captures or financial refund completion.
Frontends must not derive an active subscription from a successful button response alone.
A DSH plan is purchasable only when an active WLT product with exactly matching terms exists.

negativeInvariants: A subscription cannot be active before captured-payment proof.
A captured payment cannot activate a different client, tenant, product, currency, amount or DSH purchase.
Only one effective active subscription may exist per client.
An idempotency key cannot represent different plan, client, tenant, payment method or lifecycle action.
An activation loyalty award is append-only and exactly once per subscription purchase.
An expired or cancelled subscription cannot expose paid benefits.
MRR and active-subscriber counts come from WLT only.
A compensation claim is not complete until a WLT-owned completion reference exists.
No runtime mock, fixture or hard-coded subscriber data is allowed.
acceptance: Operator can create, independently activate, pause and archive a plan while active terms remain immutable.
Client can purchase an active matching plan and receives a WLT-bound payment session.
Activation fails before capture and succeeds exactly once after valid capture.
Activation appends the configured loyalty award exactly once in WLT.
Client benefits show only effective unexpired WLT entitlements.
Renewal creates new payment evidence and extends the period without overlap or double charge.
Cancellation removes effective benefits and exposes compensation state from WLT.
Due subscriptions expire deterministically and disappear from active benefits.
All required surfaces consume the shared controller and contain no local financial logic.
Static, database, contract, backend, frontend, security, finance and isolation checks pass on one commit.
